---
# Copyright © 2023-2026 ValidMind Inc. All rights reserved.
# Refer to the LICENSE file in the root of this repository for details.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0 AND ValidMind Commercial
title: "View settings activity"
date: last-modified
---

Use settings activity to audit organization-level settings and configuration changes made by admins in the {{< var validmind.platform >}}.

:::: {.attn}

## Prerequisites

- [x] {{< var link.login >}}
- [x] You are a [{{< fa hand >}} Customer Admin]{.bubble} or assigned another role with sufficient permissions to view organization settings activity.[^1]

::::

## About settings activity

Settings activity records changes to admin-managed configuration across your organization. Use it to review who changed a setting, when the change happened, and which settings area was affected.

Settings activity can include changes to:

- Roles and permissions.[^1]
- Users and invites.[^2]
- Organization settings.[^3]
- Groups and business units.[^4]
- Workflows.[^5]
- Record configuration, including record fields and record page layouts.[^6]
- Artifact configuration, including artifact types and custom fields.[^7]
- Custom fields.[^6]
- Governance settings, including validation guidelines, assessment options, regulations, and policies.[^8]
- Document settings, including document types, templates, and checker configuration.[^9]
- Webhook secrets and other integration secrets.[^10]
- Page layouts.[^11]

## View settings activity

To review settings activity from the Activity page:

1. In the left sidebar, click **{{< fa wifi >}} Activity**.

2. Select **Settings Activity** to view organization-level settings and configuration events.

3. Review the activity list to see who made each change, when it happened, and which settings area changed.

To review settings activity from Settings:

1. In the left sidebar, click **{{< fa gear >}} Settings**.

2. Open the **Settings Activity Log**.

3. Review the activity list to audit recent settings and configuration changes.

## Filter settings activity

Filter settings activity to narrow the audit trail to the events you need:

1. Open the settings activity view from the Activity page or Settings activity log.

2. Click **{{< fa filter >}} Filter**.

3. Add one or more filter rules, such as category, user, or date range.

4. Click **Apply Filters**.

Active filters appear as chips above the activity list. Remove individual chips or clear all filters to return to the full settings activity view.

## API access

Customer Admins can retrieve settings activity through the public audit-events API. Non-admin users receive a `403` response.

Use the public API endpoints at a high level as follows:

- `POST /organization/audit-events` — Query audit events with filters.
- `/organization/audit-events/filters` — Retrieve available filter options.

## What's next

- Use Data Exports to schedule Settings Activity CSV exports to cloud storage.[^12]
- Review model and record audit trails for activity on individual inventory records.[^13]

<!-- FOOTNOTES -->

[^1]: [Manage permissions](/guide/configuration/manage-permissions.qmd)

[^2]: [Manage users](/guide/configuration/manage-users.qmd)

[^3]: [Set up your organization](/guide/configuration/set-up-your-organization.qmd)

[^4]: [Manage groups](/guide/configuration/manage-groups.qmd)

[^5]: [Configure workflows](/guide/workflows/configure-workflows.qmd)

[^6]: [Manage inventory fields](/guide/inventory/manage-inventory-fields.qmd)

[^7]: [Manage artifact fields](/guide/validation/manage-artifact-fields.qmd)

[^8]: [Customize {{< var validmind.checker >}}](/guide/templates/customize-document-checker.qmd)

[^9]: [Manage document types](/guide/templates/manage-document-types.qmd)

[^10]: [Manage secrets](/guide/integrations/manage-secrets.qmd)

[^11]: [Customize record overview pages](/guide/inventory/customize-record-overview-page.qmd)

[^12]: [Configure data exports](/guide/integrations/configure-analytics-exports.qmd)

[^13]: [View record activity](/guide/inventory/view-record-activity.qmd)
